mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is flat or falling, discount rates and short-rate pressure on equity valuation ease, so the S&P 500 should post positive 20-day forward returns. This signal is falsified if rate cuts or declines do not coincide with above-baseline forward gains.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines or stays unchanged and the S&P 500 still records negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of such windows, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A fast month-over-month CPI acceleration above the Fed funds rate indicates inflation pressure that raises expected tightening risk and lowers near-term equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation gaps are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above a low-pressure threshold signals slowing labor income and weakening cyclical demand, so equities should underperform over the next 5 trading days as growth-sensitive investors reprice earnings risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises to at least 4.0% after increasing by 0.2pp or more over 2 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE threshold breaks are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns at least 55% of the time, or the mean return is positive, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth alongside expanding payrolls signals broad demand momentum that supports earnings revisions, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear when both consumption and employment improve together.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise month-over-month for 2 consecutive releases
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS/PAYEMS improvements are not associated with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production indicates stronger real activity and capacity utilization, which should lift earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: If positive 3-month changes in INDPRO fail to correspond to positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the average 60-day return is not above zero, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts drop sharply, it signals a weakening interest-sensitive demand channel and slower future growth, which should pressure equities over the next 60 trading days. The negative effect is strongest after a clear downshift rather than normal noise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start drops are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the conditional mean return is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: A labor-market divergence where payrolls keep rising while unemployment also rises implies firms are adding jobs slower than the labor force expands, a classic late-cycle deterioration that should weigh on stocks over the next 20 days. The signal fails when payroll growth is strong enough to offset the unemployment increase.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.2pp over 3 months while PAYEMS growth is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the unemployment-plus-soft-payrolls condition does not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDP and retail sales indicates broad demand deceleration that should compress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is strongest when both real output and consumer spending weaken together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative while RSAFS growth is below zero on a trailing 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales weakness is not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations or if the average return is positive.
